# UC-22.6.22 · Physical Security Monitoring — Camera NVR Offline or Disk Full Events

## Description

Surveillance system health events show physical monitoring controls remain operational.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes physical and sourcetypes cctv:nvr. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map devices to zones; (2) alert on any offline >15m; (3) maintenance SLA tracking. Indexes required: `index=physical`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cctv:nvr`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Genetec/Milestone syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cctv:nvr`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map devices to zones; (2) alert on any offline >15m; (3) maintenance SLA tracking.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype=cctv:nvr earliest=-7d (message="*offline*" OR message="*disk full*" OR severity>4)
| stats count by site, device_id, message
```

Understanding this SPL

**Physical Security Monitoring — Camera NVR Offline or Disk Full Events (A.7.4)** — Surveillance system health events show physical monitoring controls remain operational.

Documented **Data sources**: `cctv:nvr`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Genetec/Milestone syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: cctv:nvr. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype=cctv:nvr, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by site, device_id, message** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Timeline

## SPL

```spl
index=physical sourcetype=cctv:nvr earliest=-7d (message="*offline*" OR message="*disk full*" OR severity>4)
| stats count by site, device_id, message
```

## Visualization

Table, Timeline. Key fields: `device_id`, `message`, `site`.
